Title: Toward Nutrient-Rich Rice: Biofortification through Mineral Accumulation and Low Phytic Acid Content
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Low phytic acid 1 protein; non-enzyme/common gene affecting phytic acid accumulation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Antinutrient phosphorus storage compound; mineral nutrient
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reducing phytic acid improves mineral bioavailability by decreasing poorly bioavailable PA-bound Fe and Zn
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR knockout lowered grain phytic acid, reduced PA-bound Fe and Zn, modestly increased NA and DMA, and increased mineral accumulation, especially when combined with OsNAS2 overexpression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; phytic acid assay; LC-MS/MS for NA/DMA; ICP-MS mineral profiling; SEC-ICP-MS/ESI-MS metal speciation; field phenotyping; SEM of endosperm
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth chamber and field-grown paddy conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLpa1 knockout reduced phytic acid in brown grains by about 30-41%. 2. lpa1 grains showed lower PA-bound Fe and Zn and slightly higher NA and DMA levels. 3. lpa1 combined with RcN2 produced the highest grain mineral accumulation and improved predicted Fe/Zn bioavailability. 4. lpa1 caused agronomic penalties and strong grain chalkiness with loosely packed spherical starch granules.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful low-phytate allele for improving mineral bioavailability and generating chalky grains favorable for rice flour, but should ideally be combined with milder low-phytate alleles or optimized backgrounds to reduce yield penalties
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shorter culms, fewer tillers, reduced panicle/grain traits, lower seed-setting rate, lower hundred-grain weight, and highly chalky grains
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chalky phenotype may be advantageous for flour production despite negative effects on conventional grain quality; authors suggest combining RcN2 with alternative low-phytate alleles lacking agronomic penalties.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of nicotianamine from S-adenosyl-L-methionine, supplying the precursor pool for deoxymugineic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nicotianamine and mugineic acid family phytosiderophore biosynthesis / metal chelator biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nicotianamine to deoxymugineic acid branch of metal chelator biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for NA/DMA production in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NA and DMA chelate metals and promote mineral transport to grains; enhanced Fe and Zn bioavailability in human diet
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Brown grain and polished grain, especially endosperm-enriched mineral accumulation after polishing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene highly expressed in roots; OsRCc3 is predominantly active in root cortical cells and also phloem companion cells of nodes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root-specific overexpression increased grain NA and DMA, elevated Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Mg and K accumulation, and increased NA-/DMA-bound Fe and Zn without major agronomic penalty in RcN2 plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression under OsRCc3 promoter; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS quantification of NA/DMA; ICP-MS mineral profiling; SEC-ICP-MS/ESI-MS metal speciation; field phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth chamber and field-grown paddy conditions
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNAAT1; OsDMAS1; OsTOM1; OsIRT1; OsYSL2; OsCOPT1; OsZIP4; OsZIP5
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAS2 overexpression in roots strongly increased NA and DMA accumulation in mature brown grains. 2. RcN2 grains accumulated substantially more Fe and Zn, as well as Mn, Cu, Mg, and K, in both brown and polished grains. 3. Combining OsNAS2 overexpression with lpa1 further enhanced mineral accumulation and shifted Fe/Zn from phytate-bound to NA/DMA-bound forms. 4. Field-grown RcN2 plants showed no significant agronomic penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising biofortification gene for rice breeding and engineering, especially with tissue-specific expression to minimize growth penalties; suitable for introgression into elite cultivars and for combination with improved low-phytate alleles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant growth penalty in RcN2 alone, but combined lpa1 RcN2 lines still showed agronomic penalties attributable largely to lpa1 background
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study measured NA/DMA mainly in grains, so the relative contribution of root- versus node-derived chelators to grain accumulation remains unresolved.
